window_note: Rolling archive since April 2026. Updated weekly.

Rent control & housing wealth, house prices & SME lending, Airbnb & evictions, immigration salience, Beirut/Johannesburg property, tax redistribution.
Italy inner areas, EU structural funds, China development zones, CRA, a new framework for unintended policy consequences, Theil-decomposition inequality.

Geography of AI & the digital economy
AI adoption in Dutch municipalities, AI across European regions, post-ChatGPT skill demand, digital transformation, crowdfunding & diversification.
WFH & commuting, counterurban migration, skilled migration, disability-employment gap, college & residential stickiness.
Rural ageing & ethic of care, food insecurity, regenerative agriculture, alternative food, social-farming cooperatives.

site.author: Yang Cheng
site.tagline: A personal literature-tracking page for regional science & regional economics.
how: What this is: a rolling, searchable archive of newly published papers across 58 journals — regional science, urban/spatial economics, rural & land studies, and agricultural & environmental economics — with abstracts inline where openly available and a link to the version of record for every paper. It is a personal literature-tracking tool, shared publicly in case it is useful to others.

How it updates: every Monday morning, an automated Claude (Anthropic) session sweeps the journals' public pages and open bibliographic sources (SpringerLink, SAGE, Oxford University Press, RePEc/IDEAS, Crossref mirrors), adds new papers with a NEW badge, tags them by theme, refreshes the deep-read shortlist, and republishes this page via GitHub Pages. Each update is a Git commit, so the full history is preserved in the repository.
Self-maintenance: the site fetches missing abstracts automatically (via the owner's devices or the weekly update) and republishes every Monday. Everything here runs without manual upkeep.
disclaimers: This is a personal project maintained in my individual capacity. It does not represent the views of — and is not affiliated with, sponsored by, or endorsed by — the University of Wisconsin–Madison, any funding agency, or any other organization.
Content is collected automatically and may contain errors, omissions, or delays. All errors are my own. Always consult the linked version of record before citing.

Paper titles, abstracts, and metadata remain the copyright of their respective authors and publishers; they are reproduced here from openly accessible sources for research and educational purposes only, with a link to the publisher's version of record for every item.
This page is not affiliated with or endorsed by any of the listed journals or their publishers.
Built and maintained with Claude, Anthropic's AI assistant. Paper collection, theme tagging, and summaries are AI-assisted.
using: Search box — matches words anywhere in titles, authors, abstracts, keywords, journal names, and theme labels; multiple words must all match.
Theme chips and Journal chips — tap the row label (THEME ▸ / JOURNAL ▸) to unfold all options; one tap filters, counts shown. The small green tags on each paper filter by theme too.
Published: presets (last month / 3 / 12 months) or exact from–to dates; Update: shows only papers that arrived in one specific Monday update; NEW badges mark the latest batch.
Sort — by journal, newest/oldest publication date, or title; 'research only' hides book reviews and corrigenda; '› more' expands long abstracts.
The black DOI button opens the version of record (use institutional library access for paywalled full text); Spreadsheet (top bar) downloads the archive as .xlsx and Data as raw JSON.
adding: Add a journal: one line in data/journals.json in the GitHub repository — the next weekly update starts covering it (the journal count on this page updates automatically).
Add or adjust a theme: edit themes_taxonomy in data/overview.json (never rename existing ids).
Change any wording on this page: edit data/overview.json — or simply ask Claude in a Cowork chat.
